subtitle: In Kusumapura, a Young Man Makes the Sky Move

panels.shot: low-angle medium-wide of Aryabhata on a Kusumapura rooftop under a star-filled sky
panels.action: Aryabhata stands on a flat-roofed house in Kusumapura at night, scroll in hand, gazing up at a sky dense with stars and a bright moon over the shadowy sprawl of Gupta Pataliputra.

dialogue.text: Kusumapura, 499 CE. Under the quiet stars of Gupta Magadha, a young Āryabhaṭa dares to say: the Earth turns.

i18n.hi: कुसुमपुर, सन ४९९। गुप्त मगध के शांत तारों तले, युवा आर्यभट कहने की हिम्मत करता है: धरती घूमती है।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot over the Ganga and Pataliputra’s cityscape
panels.action: Bird’s-eye view of the Ganga curving past the long wooden palisades and tiled roofs of Pataliputra, with ferry boats and bustling quays hinting at a thriving Gupta capital.

dialogue.text: This is Kusumapura, ancient Pataliputra, on the Ganga. Under the Guptas, poets, physicians, metalworkers and sky-watchers crowd its halls and terraces.

i18n.hi: यह है कुसुमपुर, प्राचीन पाटलिपुत्र, गंगा किनारे। गुप्तों के राज में कवि, वैद्य, धातुकार और तारा निहारने वाले इसकी गलियों और महलों में उमड़ते हैं।

panels.shot: medium shot in an indoor gurukula-like hall, side light from a lattice window
panels.action: Young Aryabhata, slim and intense, sits cross-legged by an oil lamp in a scholars’ hall, palm-leaf manuscripts and counting stones arranged in careful rows before him.

dialogue.text: Āryabhaṭa is born in 476. By his early twenties, his hunger knows one field: gaṇita and jyotiḥśāstra, number and the science of the lights.

i18n.hi: आर्यभट का जन्म ४७६ में होता है। बीस की उम्र तक उसकी प्यास बस दो विद्या जानती है: गणित और ज्योतिषशास्त्र, अंक और आकाशदीपों का विज्ञान।

panels.shot: over-the-shoulder close shot on Aryabhata’s hands drawing a circle and chords on a palm leaf
panels.action: Over Aryabhata’s shoulder, his stylus scratches neat arcs and chords onto a palm-leaf circle beside rows of tiny counters on a low wooden board.

dialogue.text: He counts, he measures, he compares sky and sand-table. Circles, chords, and ratios begin to obey his patient hands.

i18n.hi: वह गिनता है, नापता है, आकाश को रेत की पाटी से मिलाता है। वृत्त, ज्या और अनुपात उसकी धीरज भरी उंगलियों के आगे झुकने लगते हैं।

panels.shot: medium-wide on a flat rooftop at twilight, horizon-level shot
panels.action: Aryabhata stands on a rooftop at twilight with a simple sighting device, watching a bright star rise as the city darkens, a faint band of the Milky Way over him.

dialogue.text: Night after night he climbs the roofs of Kusumapura, marking the rise of stars, the path of the Moon, the changing length of days.

i18n.hi: रात दर रात वह कुसुमपुर की छतों पर चढ़ता है, तारों के उगने, चाँद की चाल, दिनों की बदलती लंबाई दर्ज करता है।

panels.shot: extreme close-up on Aryabhata’s focused eyes reflecting moving stars
panels.action: Close on Aryabhata’s eyes, the reflection of slowly shifting stars gliding across his dark pupils as he stands very still in the warm night.

dialogue.text: The sky seems to whirl from east to west. But his mind asks: Is it the dome that spins, or the ground beneath his bare feet?

i18n.hi: आसमान पूरब से पश्चिम घूमता सा दिखता है। पर उसका मन पूछता है: घूमता गुम्बद है, या नंगे पाँव तले धरती?

panels.shot: dynamic medium shot on a riverbank with a moored boat, low sunset light
panels.action: On the Ganga’s edge at sunset, Aryabhata sits in a small wooden boat tied to a peg, watching the river flow while the distant bank drifts slowly in his view.

dialogue.text: One day he watches a boat slip down the Ganga. From the deck, trees on the bank seem to slide past. The eye lies; motion can belong to the watcher.

i18n.hi: एक दिन वह गंगा पर नाव को बहते देखता है। डेक से किनारे के पेड़ खिसकते लगते हैं। आँख धोखा देती है; गति कभी-कभी देखने वाले की होती है।

panels.shot: slow push-in medium close as Aryabhata looks from boat to sky, thoughtful
panels.action: Aryabhata lifts his gaze from the flowing water to the early stars, lips curled in a faint, startled smile as an idea settles.

dialogue.text: So he reasons: the Earth may be the boat, the stars the bank. The spinning sky is only the illusion seen from a turning sphere.

i18n.hi: तो वह तर्क करता है: धरती ही नाव हो सकती है, तारे ही किनारा। घूमता आसमान बस घुमती गोल धरती से दिखने वाला भ्रम है।

panels.shot: interior medium shot in a simple chamber with a low desk, lamp-lit
panels.action: In a modest chamber, Aryabhata sits at a low desk, lamp burning, as he begins to inscribe the opening verses of a new treatise on long palm leaves.

dialogue.text: At twenty-three, in the year 499, he shapes his work: the Āryabhaṭīya. One hundred and twenty-one verses, dense as iron, light as starlight.

i18n.hi: तेईस की उम्र में, सन ४९९ में, वह अपनी कृति गढ़ता है: आर्यभटीय। एक सौ इक्कीस श्लोक, लोहे जैसे घने, तारों जैसे हल्के।

panels.shot: over-the-shoulder medium close on the palm leaf as coded letter-numerals are written
panels.action: We see Aryabhata’s stylus forming Devanāgarī-like letters in carefully grouped clusters, a scheme of consonants and vowels standing in ordered rows as numerals.

dialogue.text: He gives numbers not as bare digits, but as syllables. Consonants mark tens and hundreds, vowels mark place. Poetry itself carries calculation.

i18n.hi: वह अंक सूखे अंकों की तरह नहीं, अक्षरों में देता है। व्यंजन दर्जनों-सैकड़ों बताते हैं, स्वर स्थान बताते हैं। कविता ही हिसाब ढोती है।

panels.shot: medium shot of Aryabhata standing before a small circle of students in a courtyard
panels.action: Aryabhata stands in a sunny courtyard, palm leaf in hand, as a few younger students sit cross-legged, listening, a simple gnomon casting a sharp shadow nearby.

dialogue.text: He arranges his verses in four quarters: the gaṇita of arithmetic and algebra, the kālākriyā of time, the gola of the sphere, the gaṇitapāda of geometry and chords.

i18n.hi: वह अपने श्लोक चार भागों में सजाता है: अंकगणित और बीजगणित का गणित, समय की कालक्रिया, गोल का गोला, और ज्यामिति व ज्या का गणितपाद।

panels.shot: medium shot of Aryabhata pointing to a drawn circle with marked radii on the ground
panels.action: On the dusty ground, Aryabhata has drawn a circle with radii at regular angles; he points to one chord while students gaze at numbered notches around the circle.

dialogue.text: Piece by piece he builds a jya table, values of the half-chord, what later ages will call the sine. Twenty-four entries, ready for any triangle or arc.

i18n.hi: वह टुकड़ा–टुकड़ा कर के ज्या–तालिका बनाता है, आधी ज्या के मान, जिसे आगे के युग ‘साइन’ कहेंगे। चौबीस प्रविष्टियाँ, हर त्रिभुज और चाप के लिए तैयार।

panels.shot: close shot on Aryabhata writing a verse giving the circumference of a circle
panels.action: Close on the palm leaf as Aryabhata carefully inscribes the verse encoding the ratio of circumference to diameter, numbers embedded in the flowing script.

dialogue.text: For the circle’s measure, he gives a rule. In his coded words: take 62,832 as the circumference for a diameter of 20,000. Pi, as we name it, near 3.1416.

i18n.hi: वृत्त की माप के लिए वह नियम देता है। गुप्त शब्दों में: २०,००० व्यास के लिए परिधि ६२,८३२ लो। जिसे हम पाई कहते हैं, लगभग ३.१४१६।

panels.shot: medium close on Aryabhata pausing, stylus raised, reflective
panels.action: Aryabhata pauses mid-writing, stylus lifted, his face calm but firm, the lamp’s flame steady beside him.

dialogue.text: He does not boast of perfection. He calls this value asanna, approximate. Enough for observatories and builders, honest about the limits of rule and fraction.

i18n.hi: वह पूर्णता का दम नहीं भरता। वह इस मान को ‘आसन्न’, अंदाज़ा कहता है। वेधशालाओं और राजमिस्त्रियों को काफी, और नियम–भिन्न की हदें ईमानदारी से मानता है।

panels.shot: medium wide of Aryabhata and a brahmin elder on a veranda, city and river beyond
panels.action: On a shaded veranda, an older brahmin scholar in white robes gestures toward a mural of Rahu devouring the Sun, while Aryabhata listens, holding his manuscript close.

dialogue.text: Around him, stories still speak of Rahu and Ketu, shadow demons swallowing the Sun. These tales comfort, bind ritual and sky.

i18n.hi: इधर चारों ओर राहु–केतु की कथाएँ हैं, छाया–दानव जो सूरज को निगलते हैं। ये कहानियाँ दिल बहलाती हैं, अनुष्ठान और आकाश को जोड़ती हैं।

panels.shot: low-angle medium shot as Aryabhata addresses a small learned assembly in a hall
panels.action: In a pillared hall, Aryabhata stands before seated scholars, holding up a palm leaf as light filters in, his expression respectful yet unwavering.

dialogue.speaker: aryabhata
dialogue.text: The Earth’s shadow darkens the Moon; the Moon’s shadow darkens the Sun.

i18n.hi: धरती की छाया चाँद को ढकती है; चाँद की छाया सूरज को ढकती है।

panels.shot: medium-wide of Aryabhata at a sand board tracing Sun, Earth, and Moon orbits
panels.action: On a low sand board, Aryabhata has placed three discs marked as Sun, Earth, and Moon, drawing their paths while a skeptical onlooker watches, frowning slightly.

dialogue.text: He explains eclipse as geometry. The Moon enters Earth’s cone of shadow; the Moon’s own shadow sweeps across Earth. No dragon’s head, but aligned orbits and cones.

i18n.hi: वह ग्रहण को ज्यामिति से समझाता है। चाँद धरती की छाया–शंकु में घुसता है; चाँद की अपनी छाया धरती पर लुढ़क जाती है। कोई सर्प–मुंड नहीं, बस सीधी कक्षाएँ और शंकु।

panels.shot: medium shot in the same hall, some scholars murmuring while Aryabhata remains composed
panels.action: Some elders exchange uneasy glances and whisper together at the back of the hall; Aryabhata stands still, scroll lowered but posture steady.

dialogue.text: Such words unsettle. Tradition holds, temples teach. Yet he does not curse the old tales; he simply sets shadows and spheres in their measured places.

i18n.hi: ऐसी बातें झकझोर देती हैं। परंपरा थामे है, मंदिर यही सिखाते हैं। फिर भी वह पुरानी कथाओं को कोसता नहीं; बस छाया और गोलों को उनकी नापी हुई जगहों पर रख देता है।

panels.shot: medium close on Aryabhata reciting, background hinting at a rotating Earth diagram
panels.action: Aryabhata recites from his manuscript; behind him on a plaster wall, a simple chalk circle with an arrow suggests a rotating Earth as a few students lean in.

dialogue.text: In the gola section he says it plain: the Earth revolves on its axis, turning once each sidereal day, while the stars stand almost fixed.

i18n.hi: गोला–भाग में वह साफ लिखता है: धरती अपनी धुरी पर घूमती है, हर नाक्षत्र दिवस में एक चक्कर लगाती है, और तारे लगभग स्थिर खड़े रहते हैं।

panels.shot: wide shot of a quiet monastery library as dusk falls, shelves of palm manuscripts
panels.action: Rows of palm-leaf bundles lie stacked in a dim library; a monk-like copyist carefully traces Aryabhata’s verses as dusk light fades and another lamp is lit.

dialogue.text: His little book travels. Scribes copy its verses for monasteries and courts. Some curse its brevity, some praise its daring; many puzzle for years over its verses.

i18n.hi: उसकी पतली किताब निकल पड़ती है। नकलनवीस उसके श्लोक मठों और दरबारों के लिए उतारते हैं। कुछ उसकी संक्षिप्तता को कोसते, कुछ साहस को सराहते; और कई बरसों तक इन श्लोकों पर माथा पसारते हैं।

panels.shot: time-lapse style wide panel showing centuries passing as different scholars study the same text
panels.action: In the same spot across four ghosted images, figures from later centuries—Bhāskara I, other commentators, astrologers—bend over the same style of manuscript, each adding notes in the margins.

dialogue.text: By the seventh century, Bhāskara I writes his bhāṣya, the first great commentary. Later astronomers in India and beyond carry Āryabhaṭa’s methods into their own tables.

i18n.hi: सातवीं सदी तक भास्कर प्रथम अपना भाष्य लिखते हैं, पहला बड़ा टीका। बाद के भारत–विदेश के खगोलज्ञ आर्यभट की विधियाँ अपनी–अपनी तालिकाओं में पहुँचाते हैं।

panels.shot: soft-focus medium shot of Aryabhata older, seated calmly with a student at his side
panels.action: An older Aryabhata sits beneath a tree, a young student beside him, as he gestures toward the horizon, the low Sun casting long shadows across the ground.

dialogue.speaker: aryabhata
dialogue.text: Trust what you can weigh, count, and see, even when stories differ.

i18n.hi: वज़न कर सको, गिन सको, देख सको, उसी पर भरोसा करो, चाहे कहानी कुछ और कहे।

panels.shot: epic wide shot from high above Earth, hinting at a classical Indian map beneath
panels.action: An artist’s impression of the Earth seen from above, faint gridlines and landmasses suggested, in subtle rotation, with a translucent overlay of Sanskrit numerals and chord diagrams around it.

dialogue.text: Āryabhaṭa does not end the old world; he quietly turns it. His courage lies in letting the turning Earth and measured shadow speak louder than myth, and trusting reason to follow.

i18n.hi: आर्यभट पुराना जग नहीं मिटाता; वह चुपचाप उसे घुमा देता है। उसका साहस इस में है कि घूमती धरती और नापी हुई छाया को मिथक से ऊँची आवाज़ देता है, और बुद्धि को उनके पीछे चलने देता है।

subtitle_i18n.en: In Kusumapura, a Young Man Makes the Sky Move
subtitle_i18n.hi: कुसुमपुर में, एक युवक आसमान हिला देता है
